channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mahua Moitra alleges mob attack in Nadia, names 16 BJP workers; police deny inaction
item.description: Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra has alleged that she was targeted in a mob attack in West Bengal's Nadia district, claiming that a group of protesters hurled eggs, mud, and stones at a party office...

item.title: TCN schedules maintenance outage affecting five states and parts of Niger Republic
item.description: The Transmission Company of Nigeria (TCN) has announced a planned electricity disruption that will affect parts of Kano, Katsina, Jigawa, Bauchi, and Yobe States, as well as Gazaoua in the Niger Repub...

item.title: Attorney General defends former land registrar over irregular cancellation of War Memorial Hospital lease in Nakuru
item.description: The Office of the Attorney General has moved to defend a controversial decision involving a former Nakuru Land Registrar, Eric Nyamu, in a case concerning the War Memorial Hospital land in Milimani, N...

item.title: Major African News Roundup: Sudan Conflict Allegations, Nigeria–South Africa Dispute, Flooding in Côte d'Ivoire, Rwanda Genocide Case in Germany, and Media Restrictions in Mali
item.description: A wide-ranging African news bulletin highlights several major developments across the continent. In Sudan, Amnesty International has accused the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) of serious human rights viol...

item.title: Bengaluru daycare abuse allegations: Five caregivers booked, Capgemini facility temporarily closed
item.description: A serious case of alleged child abuse has emerged from a corporate daycare facility in Bengaluru’s Brookefield area, prompting police action and an internal response from IT major Capgemini. According...

item.title: Westside Cowboy release new single ‘Pin Up Boys’ and confirm UK record store tour ahead of debut album
item.description: Manchester indie band Westside Cowboy have released their latest single ‘Pin Up Boys’, offering another preview of their forthcoming debut album ‘It Goes On’, due for release on 21 August via Island R...

item.title: Ghana calls for investigation after citizen killed during anti-immigrant unrest in Cape Town
item.description: The Government of Ghana has called for swift and decisive justice following the killing of a Ghanaian national during recent anti-immigrant protests in Cape Town, South Africa. The victim, identified ...

item.title: Kenya welcomes shift in U.S. Africa policy toward trade as talks on bilateral agreement progress
item.description: Kenya’s Prime Cabinet Secretary Musalia Mudavadi has welcomed a shift in United States policy towards Africa, noting a move away from traditional aid-based relations towards a more trade- and investme...

item.title: Debbie Harry Photographed at the Hollywood Sign in 1977 During Blondie's Early LA Shows
item.description: In March 1977, legendary singer Debbie Harry of the band Blondie was photographed by renowned music archivist Michael Ochs while standing on a hill beneath the iconic Hollywood Sign in Los Angeles, Ca...

item.title: Severe flooding in Dauphin prompts local state of emergency after record rainfall
item.description: The city of Dauphin in western Manitoba has declared a local state of emergency after experiencing severe flooding caused by intense rainfall across the Parkland region. Officials report that approxim...

item.title: Universal Limits Influencer Access to Early Screenings of Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey, Favoring Traditional Press
item.description: Universal Pictures has decided to forgo the increasingly common practice of hosting early influencer screenings for Christopher Nolan’s upcoming epic adaptation of Homer’s The Odyssey. Instead, the st...

item.title: Ken Burns reflects on America 250 and George Washington’s legacy in modern political context
item.description: This video features documentary filmmaker Ken Burns discussing themes related to the upcoming America 250 commemorations and how historical memory intersects with modern political discourse in the Uni...

item.title: Benin’s New President Romuald Wadagni Pushes Regional Diplomatic Reset with Togo and Niger
item.description: Romuald Wadagni, former Beninese finance minister, was sworn in as President of Benin on May 24, 2026, following an election widely seen as predictable due to the dominance of ruling party-aligned for...

item.title: Tanzania to overhaul legal framework to support Vision 2050 development goals
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has announced a wide-ranging plan to review and amend existing laws in order to align them with the country’s Vision 2050 development blueprint. This initiative is part of a...

item.title: Mick Jagger praises Sam Fender’s music and discusses younger artists he has been listening to
item.description: Mick Jagger has expressed admiration for Sam Fender, revealing that he has been enjoying the younger British musician’s work and describing himself as having ‘Sam Fender moments’ recently. Speaking to...

item.title: Heavy Canada Day rainfall floods Ottawa roads and causes widespread power outages
item.description: Severe thunderstorms struck Ottawa on Canada Day, bringing intense rainfall that led to widespread flooding and power disruptions across the city. According to reports, between approximately 70 mm and...

item.title: Village People lead singer Victor Willis dies at 74 after brief illness
item.description: The global music community is mourning the death of Victor Willis, the lead singer and founding member of the iconic disco group Village People. According to reports, Willis passed away on Tuesday, Ju...

item.title: Tanzania SOEs achieve record Sh1.3 trillion dividend following efficiency reforms
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has received a record Sh1.327 trillion in dividends and contributions from state-owned enterprises and minority-held companies for the 2025/2026 financial year, marking a 30...

item.title: British expat highlights how Cape Town homes feel colder indoors than outside during winter
item.description: A British actress and content creator living in Cape Town has sparked amused reactions online after pointing out how cold South African homes can feel during winter. Tara Berwin shared a video of her ...

item.title: Anthropic hires Berkeley CS chair Jelani Nelson as part of ongoing AI talent expansion
item.description: Jelani Nelson, a leading theoretical computer scientist and chair of UC Berkeley’s electrical engineering and computer science division, has taken a leave of absence from the university to join Anthro...
